entries.description: Foundational ethical monotheism and moral code (Ten Commandments, Torah ethics) that profoundly influenced Western law, human rights, and civilization
entries.causation_evidence: Hebrew Bible and historical impact on Western legal and moral systems

entries.description: Development of systematic religious education, literacy, and scholarship (yeshivas, Talmudic study) that preserved knowledge through centuries of diaspora
entries.causation_evidence: Historical development of Jewish education systems

entries.description: Re-establishment of the State of Israel as a Jewish homeland after 2,000 years of diaspora and the Holocaust
entries.causation_evidence: UN Partition Plan and Israeli Declaration of Independence

entries.description: Disproportionate contributions to science, medicine, technology, Nobel Prizes, and intellectual life relative to population size
entries.causation_evidence: Nobel Prize statistics and modern Jewish contributions in STEM

entries.description: Moral and intellectual leadership in post-Holocaust human rights frameworks and the concept of 'Never Again'
entries.causation_evidence: Influence on Universal Declaration of Human Rights and international law

entries.description: Development of rabbinic Judaism, the Talmud, and a portable legal-ethical system that enabled Jewish survival through centuries of exile
entries.causation_evidence: Compilation of the Talmud and rabbinic literature

entries.description: Global Jewish charitable networks and philanthropy supporting education, health, disaster relief, and social causes worldwide
entries.causation_evidence: Major Jewish foundations and organizations

entries.description: Israeli occupation of Palestinian territories, settlement expansion, and policies ruled illegal by the International Court of Justice
entries.causation_evidence: ICJ Advisory Opinion (July 2024) and UN reports

entries.description: Gaza military operations resulting in high civilian casualties, widespread destruction, and humanitarian crisis
entries.causation_evidence: UN OCHA, HRW, Amnesty International, and Gaza Health Ministry data 2023–2026

entries.description: Treatment of Palestinians under occupation, including movement restrictions, home demolitions, and settler violence
entries.causation_evidence: UN Human Rights Council, B'Tselem, and HRW reports

entries.description: Ultra-Orthodox influence leading to gender segregation, exclusion of women, and resistance to secular education in some communities
entries.causation_evidence: Israeli Supreme Court cases and reports on Haredi communities

entries.description: Historical and contemporary religious extremism, including settler violence and messianic nationalism in the West Bank
entries.causation_evidence: UN OCHA reports on settler attacks and Israeli security data

entries.description: Ongoing discrimination against non-Orthodox Jews, women, and converts in matters of marriage, divorce, and religious status in Israel
entries.causation_evidence: Israeli Supreme Court rulings and reports on Chief Rabbinate policies

entries.description: Role of certain Jewish organizations and lobbying groups in shaping U.S. foreign policy in ways that prioritize Israeli interests over broader American ones
entries.causation_evidence: Criticism from Mearsheimer/Walt and others

entries.description: Historical involvement in financial practices and moneylending that contributed to anti-Semitic stereotypes and pogroms
entries.causation_evidence: Medieval European history and resulting antisemitism

entries.description: Treatment of Palestinian citizens of Israel and Arab residents under certain Israeli policies influenced by Jewish religious nationalism
entries.causation_evidence: Adalah, B'Tselem, and HRW reports on discrimination inside Israel

entries.description: Ongoing ultra-Orthodox influence in Israel leading to exemption from military service, resistance to secular education, and gender segregation
entries.causation_evidence: Israeli political and court records

entries.description: Historical expulsions, ghettoization, and persecution of Jews in Christian Europe, partly enabled by religious teachings
entries.causation_evidence: Centuries of European antisemitism rooted in religious sources

entries.description: Modern instances of Jewish religious extremism, including price-tag attacks and messianic settler violence in the West Bank
entries.causation_evidence: UN OCHA and Israeli security reports on settler violence

entries.description: Resilience and cultural preservation through 2,000 years of diaspora, persecution, and exile
entries.causation_evidence: Jewish historical continuity

entries.description: Ethical contributions including tikkun olam (repairing the world) and emphasis on justice, charity, and intellectual debate
entries.causation_evidence: Core Jewish teachings and modern Jewish activism

entries.description: Major role in the development of modern medicine, psychology, law, economics, and social sciences by Jewish thinkers
entries.causation_evidence: Disproportionate Nobel Prizes and academic achievements

entries.description: Strong tradition of debate, questioning authority, and intellectual rigor (Talmudic dialectic)
entries.causation_evidence: Talmudic method and Jewish scholarly culture

entries.description: Survival and cultural renaissance after the Holocaust, including the revival of Hebrew as a living language
entries.causation_evidence: Post-1948 Jewish cultural and linguistic revival
